name: Guess, John
resolved_employer: Houston Museum of African American Culture (HMAAC) — CEO/founder
evidence: John Guess Jr., a native Houstonian and prominent arts/culture philanthropist, is CEO of the Houston Museum of African American Culture and sits on boards of Contemporary Arts Museum Houston and Houston Arts Alliance. SW-Houston 77096 zip, prolific Democratic donor (264 FEC gifts, lean 1.0) supporting Travis Co. commissioner Jeff Travillion fits the profile.

name: Carter, Darryl
evidence: No zip, occupation, or employer on record and 'Darryl Carter' is a common name; a $500 gift to Travillion and 28 FEC donations are insufficient to corroborate a specific individual. Not identifiable.

evidence: 'J Ancira Strategies' is J. Ancira Strategies PLLC, a Taylor, TX public-affairs/lobbying/political-consulting firm run by Jesse Ancira Jr. (former Taylor mayor and chief of staff to House Speaker Joe Straus). Registered Texas lobby client. Entity donor to George Morales.

name: Rather, Jean
resolved_employer: Independent visual artist (painter/collage); wife of journalist Dan Rather (d. Nov 2024, Austin)
evidence: Jean (Goebel) Rather, born Smithville, TX 1935, was a painter and collage artist and former Vice Chair of NYC's Art Commission; wife of Dan Rather for 67 years, she lived in Austin and died there in Nov 2024. Downtown Austin 78701, gift to Brigid Shea in 2022 during her Austin residency.

name: Latson, Ed
resolved_employer: Austin Regional Manufacturers Association (ARMA) — founder/CEO; later CEO of Opportunity Austin
evidence: Ed Latson founded and led ARMA, an Austin manufacturing trade/advocacy association, matching the record's 'CEO / ARMA'; in Oct 2023 he became CEO of Opportunity Austin (regional economic development). Austin 78759, gift to Andy Brown 2022. Trade-association exec, not himself a defense contractor.

evidence: Lance Richard Sallis is a licensed Austin real estate broker (TREC #407246, since 1990) and President of Sallis Commercial Real Estate, Inc., at 3105 Crowheart Cove, Austin 78746 — exact zip match to the record's 'Real Estate / Self-Employed / 78746'.

name: Stitt, Mike

evidence: Mike Stitt was named CEO of Austin's Easy Tiger (bake shop/beer garden) in 2020; 'ET Holdings' on the record almost certainly = Easy Tiger's holding company (ET initials + his documented Easy Tiger role). evidence: Jill van Voorhis is Principal at Stratecom Advisors, LLC, an Austin-area public-affairs/business-development consultancy that also runs political campaigns, matching record 'Strategy / Self'. Austin 78746, prolific Dem donor (387 FEC gifts, lean 1.0), gift to Andy Brown 2021.
